summary: The Goshen Township Board of Zoning Appeals held a public hearing on case BZA 2023-008, a variance request for a proposed Domino's restaurant at the intersection of State Route 28 and 132. The applicant sought relief from front yard setback requirements (30 feet instead of 50 feet along the north property line) and parking standards (20 spaces instead of the required 23). Staff recommended approval citing the constraints imposed by a 100-year floodplain and creek on the property. The board approved the variances.
decisions: Approved meeting minutes from previous hearing
Granted variance reducing required parking spaces from 23 to 20 for proposed Domino's restaurant (BZA 2023-008)
action_items: Applicant (Caliber Engineering / Domino's) to address signage package separately under sign ordinance requirements
Staff to confirm the second variance (front yard setback) resolution was handled or was not necessary as a separate vote
key_amounts: 50-foot front yard setback required; 30-foot setback proposed (20-foot reduction requested along north property line)
23 parking spaces required; 20 spaces proposed (3-space reduction)
1 parking space per 100 square feet required for restaurants in B2 district
20-foot minimum landscaping buffer required along south and west property lines adjacent to residential districts
notable_topics: Variance request for proposed Domino's restaurant (BZA 2023-008)
Front yard setback variance due to 100-year floodplain and creek constraints
Parking space reduction variance for carry-out focused restaurant with limited seating
Traffic and driveway access concerns near State Route 132 intersection
Demolition of existing vacant bank building
Retaining wall proposed on south portion of property for floodplain mitigation
Welcome of new board member John
Open alternate position on the BZA board
attendees: Mr. Holmes (absent/not responding to roll call)
notes: Mr. Holmes appears to have been absent as he did not respond during roll call or any votes. The board appeared to vote on the parking variance but the front yard setback variance vote was less clear in the transcript — there was discussion at the end about whether a separate public statement or resolution was needed for the other variance. The applicant had previously considered a different site (referred to as 'Patricia's') before returning to this location. Next meeting scheduled for July 17, 2023.
title: BZA 6-20-2023
